situational_context: Running comeback week 4, consolidated at 5 km. Run #5 (May 23) showed clear cardiac adaptation: avg HR 136 / suffer 19 vs Run #4 avg HR 140 / suffer 26 at identical distance. Chronic load 18.7 (declining from 22.3 as cycling volume reduced in favor of structured running). E-bike commuting continues on sur-site days (~38 km round trip, suffer 8-12). Weight stable at 103.84 kg. ACWR 0.89 — safe rebuild zone. Readiness HIGH today (77). No red flags active. Next milestone: confirm 5 km consolidation with Run #6 (May 28), then evaluate distance progression to 6 km.

recommendations.text: Execute today's vélo endurance Z2 (50 min, FC 120-140 bpm). Readiness is HIGH (77) — best training window this week. If any residual lower-limb soreness from Run #5 (May 23), default to 30 min Z1.
recommendations.measurable: Session completed with avg HR 120-140 bpm and training load 13-17

recommendations.text: Maintain strict 72h gap before Run #6 (Wednesday). The Tuesday rest is non-negotiable for tissue recovery at 104 kg body weight — tendons need time between impact sessions regardless of cardiovascular readiness.
recommendations.measurable: No running between today and May 28

recommendations.text: Address protein deficit before nutritionist appointment (May 28). Weekly average is 133 g/day vs 150 g target. Prioritize distribution: aim for 35-40 g per meal (4 meals) rather than concentrating at lunch.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Run #6 avg HR should be ≤138 bpm at 5 km. If >140, flag potential overreach.
markers_to_watch.reason: Runs #4→#5 showed clear HR decline (140→136). Continuation of trend confirms tissue and cardiac adaptation.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Currently 18.7 and declining. This week's 4 sessions should stabilize or begin reversing the decline.
markers_to_watch.reason: Declining from 22.3 (May 14) as cycling volume reduced. Need to arrest decline to maintain fitness base.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Flag if ≥2 nights <6h in next 7 days
markers_to_watch.reason: One poor night (5.95h on May 24) in last 7. Baby-related disruption normal but accumulation risks recovery.
